tEn: Supervising strong learners by amplifying weak experts
tZh: 通过增强弱专家来监督强学习者
sEn: Iterated Amplification trains complex behaviors by combining solutions to easier subproblems without an external reward function.
sZh: 迭代放大通过组合较易子问题的解决方案来训练复杂行为，无需外部奖励函数。
absEn: Many real world learning tasks involve complex or hard-to-specify objectives, and using an easier-to-specify proxy can lead to poor performance or misaligned behavior. One solution is to have humans provide a training signal by demonstrating or judging performance, but this approach fails if the task is too complicated for a human to directly evaluate. We propose Iterated Amplification, an alternative training strategy which progressively builds up a training signal for difficult problems by combining solutions to easier subproblems. Iterated Amplification is closely related to Expert Iteration (Anthony et al., 2017; Silver et al., 2017), except that it uses no external reward function. We present results in algorithmic environments, showing that Iterated Amplification can efficiently learn complex behaviors.
absZh: 许多现实世界的学习任务涉及复杂或难以指定的目标，使用更容易指定的代理目标可能导致性能不佳或行为失调。一种解决方案是由人类通过演示或判断表现来提供训练信号，但如果任务过于复杂以至于人类无法直接评估，这种方法就会失败。我们提出了迭代放大，这是一种替代训练策略，通过组合较易子问题的解决方案，逐步为困难问题构建训练信号。迭代放大与专家迭代（Anthony等人，2017；Silver等人，2017）密切相关，区别在于它不使用外部奖励函数。我们在算法环境中展示了结果，表明迭代放大可以高效地学习复杂行为。

contrib.en: Proposes Iterated Amplification, a training strategy that builds training signals by combining solutions to easier subproblems without an external reward function.
contrib.zh: 提出迭代放大（Iterated Amplification）训练策略，通过组合较易子问题的解来构建训练信号，无需外部奖励函数。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that Iterated Amplification can efficiently learn complex behaviors in algorithmic environments, achieving performance close to supervised learning with ground truth.
contrib.zh: 在算法环境中证明迭代放大能高效学习复杂行为，性能接近使用真实标签的监督学习。

contrib.en: Shows that Iterated Amplification requires orders of magnitude fewer expert demonstrations than direct supervised learning, reducing sample complexity.
contrib.zh: 表明迭代放大所需的专家示范数量比直接监督学习少几个数量级，降低了样本复杂度。

contrib.en: Introduces a human predictor model to reduce the burden on human experts, enabling scalable training with non-stationary distributions.
contrib.zh: 引入人类预测模型以减轻人类专家负担，实现在非平稳分布下的可扩展训练。

contrib.en: Provides a framework for training AI systems on tasks beyond human scale, where direct human evaluation is infeasible.
contrib.zh: 为训练超出人类规模的任务提供框架，这些任务中人类直接评估不可行。

contrib.en: Discusses the key assumption of decomposability and argues that human coordination of multiple AI copies can outperform a single copy.
contrib.zh: 讨论可分解性的关键假设，并论证人类协调多个AI副本可以胜过单个副本。

limits.en: Experiments use algorithmic tasks with hand-coded decompositions, not real-world human tasks.
limits.zh: 实验使用算法任务和手工编码的分解，而非真实世界的人类任务。

limits.en: Assumes tasks are decomposable into simpler subproblems, which may not hold for all complex real-world tasks.
limits.zh: 假设任务可分解为更简单的子问题，这未必适用于所有复杂的真实世界任务。

limits.en: Requires a broad question distribution covering all subquestions, which may be difficult to design in practice.
limits.zh: 需要覆盖所有子问题的广泛问题分布，实践中可能难以设计。

limits.en: Training with amplification requires about twice the computation per question compared to supervised learning.
limits.zh: 与监督学习相比，使用放大训练每个问题需要大约两倍的计算量。

limits.en: The method has not been validated on messy real-world tasks beyond synthetic domains.
limits.zh: 该方法尚未在合成领域之外的复杂真实世界任务上得到验证。

items.en: Many real world learning tasks involve complex or hard-to-specify objectives, and using an easier-to-specify proxy can lead to poor performance or misaligned behavior. One solution is to have humans provide a training signal by demonstrating or judging performance, but this approach fails if the task is too complicated for a human to directly evaluate. We propose Iterated Amplification, an alternative training strategy which progressively builds up a training signal for difficult problems by combining solutions to easier subproblems. Iterated Amplification is closely related to Expert Iteration (Anthony et al., 2017; Silver et al., 2017b), except that it uses no external reward function. We present results in algorithmic environments, showing that Iterated Amplification can efficiently learn complex behaviors.
items.zh: 许多现实世界的学习任务涉及复杂或难以指定的目标，使用较易指定的代理目标可能导致性能不佳或行为失调。一种解决方案是由人类通过演示或评判表现来提供训练信号，但如果任务过于复杂以至于人类无法直接评估，这种方法就会失效。我们提出迭代放大（Iterated Amplification），这是一种替代训练策略，通过组合较简单子问题的解决方案逐步构建困难问题的训练信号。迭代放大与专家迭代（Expert Iteration）密切相关（Anthony 等，2017；Silver 等，2017b），区别在于它不使用外部奖励函数。我们在算法环境中展示了结果，表明迭代放大能够高效学习复杂行为。

items.en: If we want to train an ML system to perform a task, we need to be able to evaluate how well it is doing. Whether our training signal takes the form of labels, rewards, or something else entirely, we need some way to generate that signal.
items.zh: 如果我们想训练一个机器学习系统执行任务，就需要能够评估其表现。无论训练信号是标签、奖励还是其他形式，我们都需要某种方式来生成该信号。

items.en: If our goal can be evaluated automatically, such as winning a game of Go, or if we have an algorithm that can generate examples of correct behavior, then generating a training signal is trivial. In these cases we might say that there is an “algorithmic” training signal.
items.zh: 如果我们的目标可以自动评估，例如赢得围棋比赛，或者我们有算法能生成正确行为的示例，那么生成训练信号就很简单。在这些情况下，我们可以说存在“算法性”训练信号。

items.en: Unfortunately, most useful tasks don’t have an algorithmic training signal. So in current applications of machine learning, humans often provide the training signal. This can be done by having a human demonstrate the task, for example labeling an image or teleoperating a robot, or by learning a reward function from human judgments. For these classes of tasks, we could say there is a “human” training signal.
items.zh: 不幸的是，大多数有用的任务没有算法性训练信号。因此在当前的机器学习应用中，人类通常提供训练信号。这可以通过让人类演示任务（例如标注图像或远程操作机器人）或从人类判断中学习奖励函数来实现。对于这类任务，我们可以说存在“人类”训练信号。

items.en: However, there are harder tasks for which we can’t compute demonstrations or rewards even with human assistance, and for which we currently have no clear method to get a meaningful training signal. Consider making economic policy decisions, advancing the scientific frontier, or managing the security of a large network of computers. Some of these tasks are “beyond human scale” – a single human can’t perform them and can’t make sense of their massive observation space well enough to judge the behavior of an agent. It may be possible for a human to judge performance in the very long run (for example, by looking at economic growth over several years), but such long-term feedback is very slow to learn from. We currently have no way to learn how to perform such tasks much better than a human.
items.zh: 然而，有些更困难的任务，即使有人类协助，我们也无法计算演示或奖励，并且目前没有明确的方法来获得有意义的训练信号。例如制定经济政策决策、推进科学前沿或管理大型计算机网络的安全。其中一些任务“超出人类规模”——单个人类无法执行，也无法理解其庞大的观测空间以判断智能体的行为。人类或许能在长期内评判表现（例如通过观察几年的经济增长），但这种长期反馈的学习速度非常慢。目前我们无法学习如何比人类更好地执行此类任务。

items.en: The overall situation is depicted in Table 1, which shows six different combinations of training signal source and problem formulation (supervised learning or RL). The bulk of ML practice operates in the top center box (supervised learning from human labels), the bottom left box (RL with a scripted reward), and sometimes the top left box (supervised learning of algorithms). The bottom center box (RL from a human training signal) is beginning to be explored, and includes inverse reinforcement learning (Ng and Russell, 2000; Abbeel and Ng, 2004; Finn et al., 2016) and RL from human feedback (Knox and Stone, 2009; Pilarski et al., 2011; MacGlashan et al., 2017; Christiano et al., 2017). At present there seems to be no general method to handle problems in the bottom right or top right.
items.zh: 整体情况如表1所示，其中列出了训练信号来源和问题形式（监督学习或强化学习）的六种不同组合。机器学习实践的主体位于顶部中间框（来自人类标签的监督学习）、左下框（具有脚本化奖励的强化学习），有时也在左上框（算法的监督学习）。底部中间框（来自人类训练信号的强化学习）开始被探索，包括逆强化学习（Ng 和 Russell，2000；Abbeel 和 Ng，2004；Finn 等，2016）和基于人类反馈的强化学习（Knox 和 Stone，2009；Pilarski 等，2011；MacGlashan 等，2017；Christiano 等，2017）。目前似乎没有通用方法来处理右下或右上框的问题。

items.en: It seems desirable to expand the range of tasks for which we can get a training signal, for two reasons. First, it would enable ML systems to perform new tasks. SL and RL are very powerful methods when we can get a training signal, so making them applicable to tasks that humans can’t directly judge or perform could have a big impact. Second, better specification of complex goals and targets may be vital to building robustly beneficial AI systems. In practice, when an accurate training signal would be “beyond human scale,” we often instead find a short-term proxy that is correlated with what we want. But aggressively optimizing that proxy can lead to pathological behavior (Lehman et al., 2018; Amodei and Clark, 2016; Amodei et al., 2016), an example of Goodhart’s Law.111“When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure” For example, we might find that user-reported satisfaction (which we can easily measure) is a good proxy for long-term benefit to society (which is very complicated), but if we maximize it with RL our agent may maintain fraudulent appearances or effectively manipulate users into providing high ratings. At large scales this kind of pathology could lead to systemic crashes, and a mismatch between proxies and our real preferences is a major source of concerns about the safety of future powerful AI systems (Bostrom, 2014).
items.zh: 扩展我们能够获得训练信号的任务范围似乎是可取的，原因有二。首先，这将使机器学习系统能够执行新任务。当我们能获得训练信号时，监督学习和强化学习是非常强大的方法，因此将它们应用于人类无法直接判断或执行的任务可能会产生重大影响。其次，更好地指定复杂目标和指标可能对构建稳健有益的AI系统至关重要。在实践中，当准确的训练信号“超出人类规模”时，我们通常寻找与我们想要的相关的短期代理。但激进地优化该代理可能导致病态行为（Lehman 等，2018；Amodei 和 Clark，2016；Amodei 等，2016），这是古德哈特定律的一个例子。111“当一项指标成为目标时，它就不再是一个好指标。”例如，我们可能发现用户报告的满意度（易于测量）是长期社会效益（非常复杂）的良好代理，但如果我们通过强化学习最大化它，我们的智能体可能维持虚假表象或有效操纵用户提供高评分。在大规模下，这种病态可能导致系统性崩溃，代理与我们真实偏好之间的不匹配是未来强大AI系统安全担忧的主要来源（Bostrom，2014）。

items.en: In this paper we propose a general framework for building up a training signal on complex tasks by decomposing them (with AI assistance) into simpler tasks for which we have a human or algorithmic training signal. In our experiments we apply the framework with a number of simplifications (see Section 4.3) to relatively simple tasks, as a first step towards addressing the problems described above.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一个通用框架，通过将复杂任务分解（借助AI辅助）为更简单的任务（我们拥有人类或算法训练信号）来构建训练信号。在我们的实验中，我们将该框架进行了一些简化（见第4.3节）应用于相对简单的任务，作为解决上述问题的第一步。

items.en: We propose a new method, Iterated Amplification, for a human expert H𝐻H to train an ML agent X𝑋X. Rather than having H𝐻H demonstrate or evaluate the target behavior on their own, we allow them to invoke several copies of the current agent X𝑋X to help them. We write AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) for the composite system, consisting of H𝐻H and several copies of X𝑋X working together to solve a problem. The agent X𝑋X then learns from AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) in the same way that it would traditionally learn from H𝐻H alone.
items.zh: 我们提出了一种新方法，迭代放大（Iterated Amplification），用于人类专家H训练机器学习智能体X。我们不是让H单独演示或评估目标行为，而是允许他们调用当前智能体X的多个副本协助他们。我们将复合系统记为AmplifyH(X)，由H和多个X副本协作解决问题。然后智能体X从AmplifyH(X)中学习，就像传统上从H单独学习一样。

items.en: To instantiate this framework we make three design decisions:
items.zh: 为了实例化这个框架，我们做了三个设计决策：

items.en: What set of tasks do we train X𝑋X to solve? In order for X𝑋X to be a useful assistant, we need to choose a sufficiently broad set of tasks. In this article, we will focus on question-answering.
items.zh: 我们训练X解决哪些任务集？为了使X成为有用的助手，我们需要选择足够广泛的任务集。在本文中，我们将专注于问答。

items.en: How do we construct AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right)? In this article, we focus on delegation: AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) answers a question Q𝑄Q by having H𝐻H identify a sequence of useful subquestions, using X𝑋X to compute a subanswer to each subquestion, and having H𝐻H decide how to answer Q𝑄Q after seeing the subanswers.
items.zh: 我们如何构建AmplifyH(X)？在本文中，我们专注于委派：AmplifyH(X)通过让H识别一系列有用的子问题，使用X计算每个子问题的子答案，并让H在看到子答案后决定如何回答问题Q。

items.en: How does X𝑋X learn from AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right)? In this article, we focus on supervised learning: X𝑋X is an autoregressive model trained to predict AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right)’s output. Future work could instead use imitation learning, or use AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) to define a reward function that X𝑋X maximizes with RL.
items.zh: X如何从AmplifyH(X)学习？在本文中，我们专注于监督学习：X是一个自回归模型，训练以预测AmplifyH(X)的输出。未来的工作可以使用模仿学习，或使用AmplifyH(X)定义奖励函数，X通过强化学习最大化该函数。

items.en: Initially X𝑋X behaves randomly, so AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) is essentially equivalent to H𝐻H and we are effectively learning from an expert. Over time the agent X𝑋X becomes more powerful and the role of the expert transitions into “coordinating” several copies of X𝑋X to solve the problem better than a single copy could solve it. (Once X𝑋X is very sophisticated, even tasks like “identify a useful subquestion” might be delegated.) As long as it is possible for multiple agents to collaboratively solve problems more effectively than a single agent (perhaps using human expertise to coordinate their efforts), then AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) can outperform X𝑋X and hence provide a useful training signal. We discuss this assumption in Section 5.
items.zh: 最初X行为随机，因此AmplifyH(X)基本上等同于H，我们实际上是从专家学习。随着时间的推移，智能体X变得更强大，专家的角色转变为“协调”多个X副本以比单个副本更好地解决问题。（一旦X非常复杂，甚至像“识别有用的子问题”这样的任务也可能被委派。）只要多个智能体协作解决问题比单个智能体更有效（可能利用人类专业知识协调他们的努力），那么AmplifyH(X)就能超越X，从而提供有用的训练信号。我们在第5节讨论这一假设。

items.en: The human must be involved in this process because there is no external objective to guide learning—the objective is implicit in the way that the human coordinates the copies of X𝑋X. For example, we have no external measure of what constitutes a “good” answer to a question, this notion is only implicit in how a human decides to combine the answers to subquestions (which usually involves both facts and value judgments). Our goal is for X𝑋X to learn the goal at the same time that it learns to behave competently. This is in contrast with the alternative approach of specifying a reward function and then training a capable agent to maximize that reward function.
items.zh: 人类必须参与这个过程，因为没有外部目标来指导学习——目标隐含在人类协调X副本的方式中。例如，我们没有外部衡量标准来定义什么是问题的“好”答案，这个概念仅隐含在人类如何组合子问题的答案中（通常涉及事实和价值判断）。我们的目标是让X在学习行为胜任的同时学习目标。这与指定奖励函数然后训练有能力的智能体最大化该奖励函数的替代方法形成对比。

items.en: In Section 2 we describe Iterated Amplification and our implementation in more detail. In Section 3 we compare our approach to prior work. In Section 4 we describe our experimental results, showing that Iterated Amplification can be stable and efficient despite the non-stationary training signal and lack of external objective. In Section 5 we explain why we believe that decomposability is a realistic assumption for complex tasks in the real world.
items.zh: 在第2节中，我们更详细地描述了迭代放大及其实现。在第3节中，我们将我们的方法与先前工作进行比较。在第4节中，我们描述了实验结果，表明尽管训练信号非平稳且缺乏外部目标，迭代放大仍能稳定高效。在第5节中，我们解释了为什么我们认为可分解性是现实世界中复杂任务的合理假设。

items.en: In order to reduce the burden on the human expert H𝐻H, we train a “human predictor” H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime}, and use this predictor to generate training data rather than consulting H𝐻H directly. That is, we train H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} to imitate the role of H𝐻H when computing AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right), and we train X𝑋X using AmplifyH′⁡(X)superscriptAmplifysuperscriptH′𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H^{\prime}}}\left(X\right) rather than using AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) directly.
items.zh: 为了减轻人类专家H的负担，我们训练了一个“人类预测器”H'，并使用该预测器生成训练数据，而不是直接咨询H。也就是说，我们训练H'模仿H在计算AmplifyH(X)时的角色，并使用AmplifyH'(X)而不是直接使用AmplifyH(X)来训练X。

items.en: Because H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} is only learning how to identify subquestions and combine subanswers, rather than solving an entire task, we expect to train it with much less data.
items.zh: 因为H'只学习如何识别子问题和组合子答案，而不是解决整个任务，我们期望用更少的数据训练它。

items.en: Note that H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} needs to predict how H𝐻H will respond to subanswers provided by X𝑋X. Because X𝑋X is changing, this distribution is non-stationary, and so we need to continuously update H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} throughout the training process.
items.zh: 注意H'需要预测H将如何响应X提供的子答案。由于X在变化，这个分布是非平稳的，因此我们需要在整个训练过程中持续更新H'。

items.en: We train an agent X𝑋X to answer questions from some distribution 𝒟𝒟\mathcal{D}.
items.zh: 我们训练智能体X回答来自某个分布D的问题。

items.en: Our training process, depicted in Fig. 1, involves running four processes in parallel:
items.zh: 我们的训练过程如图1所示，涉及并行运行四个进程：

items.en: We repeatedly sample a question Q∼𝒟similar-to𝑄𝒟Q\sim\mathcal{D}, use AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) to answer that question, and record every decision made by H𝐻H during the process. That is, H𝐻H finds a subquestion Q1subscript𝑄1Q_{1} that would help them answer Q𝑄Q, and we compute the answer A1=X​(Q1)subscript𝐴1𝑋subscript𝑄1A_{1}=X(Q_{1}). We repeat this process k𝑘k times, where k𝑘k is a fixed parameter, and then H𝐻H computes an answer A𝐴A. We store the transcript τ=(Q,Q1,A1,…,Qk,Ak,A)𝜏𝑄subscript𝑄1subscript𝐴1…subscript𝑄𝑘subscript𝐴𝑘𝐴\tau=\left(Q,Q_{1},A_{1},\ldots,Q_{k},A_{k},A\right).
items.zh: 我们反复从分布𝒟中采样问题Q，使用Amplifyᴴ(X)来回答该问题，并记录H在此过程中做出的每一个决策。即，H找到一个有助于回答Q的子问题Q₁，然后我们计算答案A₁ = X(Q₁)。我们重复此过程k次，其中k是一个固定参数，然后H计算最终答案A。我们存储记录τ = (Q, Q₁, A₁, …, Qₖ, Aₖ, A)。

items.en: We train a model H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} to predict the decisions made by H𝐻H in each of these transcripts, i.e. to predict subquestions Qisubscript𝑄𝑖Q_{i} and final answers A𝐴A.
items.zh: 我们训练一个模型H'来预测H在每个记录中做出的决策，即预测子问题Qᵢ和最终答案A。

items.en: We repeatedly sample a question Q∼𝒟similar-to𝑄𝒟Q\sim\mathcal{D}, use AmplifyH′⁡(X)superscriptAmplifysuperscriptH′𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H^{\prime}}}\left(X\right) to answer that question, and record the resulting (Q,A)𝑄𝐴(Q,A) pairs.
items.zh: 我们反复从分布𝒟中采样问题Q，使用Amplifyᴴ'(X)来回答该问题，并记录得到的(Q, A)对。

items.en: X𝑋X is trained by supervised learning on these (Q,A)𝑄𝐴(Q,A) pairs.
items.zh: X通过监督学习在这些(Q, A)对上进行训练。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Schematic of our Iterated Amplification implementation.
items.capZh: 图1：我们迭代放大实现的示意图。

items.en: The behavior of the agent X𝑋X develops over the course of training:
items.zh: 智能体X的行为在训练过程中逐步发展：

items.en: Initially X𝑋X answers questions randomly. When the human asks subquestions they frequently receive incoherent or useless subanswers,
items.zh: 最初X随机回答问题。当人类询问子问题时，经常收到不连贯或无用的子答案。

items.en: The human is able to answer some questions without any help from X𝑋X, and eventually X𝑋X learns to copy these simple answers.
items.zh: 人类能够在没有X帮助的情况下回答一些问题，最终X学会了复制这些简单答案。

items.en: Once X𝑋X is able to provide simple answers, the human is able to provide slightly better answers by breaking them into simple pieces. Then X𝑋X learns to provide slightly better answers.
items.zh: 一旦X能够提供简单答案，人类就能通过将问题分解为简单部分来提供稍好的答案。然后X学会提供稍好的答案。

items.en: This process continues, with X𝑋X gradually expanding the set of queries it can answer and gradually improving the answers it provides. At each point in training, AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) is modestly smarter than X working on its own, and X𝑋X chases this moving target.
items.zh: 这个过程持续进行，X逐渐扩展其能回答的查询集合并逐步改进其提供的答案。在训练的每个阶段，Amplifyᴴ(X)都比独立工作的X稍微聪明一些，而X则追逐这个移动的目标。

items.en: If all goes well, at the end of this process we’re left with an agent that “approximates” the behavior of an exponentially large team of copies of H𝐻H. The hierarchical decomposition itself is discarded as an artifact of training, and the actual procedure learned by the agent will generally not mirror the structure used in training.
items.zh: 如果一切顺利，在这个过程结束时，我们将得到一个“近似”于指数级规模的人类副本团队行为的智能体。层次分解本身作为训练产物被丢弃，智能体实际学到的过程通常不会反映训练中使用的结构。

items.en: In practice, questions often take the form of a very large context (e.g. a hundred-page design document) and a relatively small context-conditional question (e.g. “what are the largest risks when implementing this design?”). In particular, this is the case in the experiments reported in Section 4.
items.zh: 在实践中，问题通常表现为非常大的上下文（例如一份百页的设计文档）和一个相对较小的上下文条件问题（例如“实施此设计时最大的风险是什么？”）。特别是，在第4节报告的实验中就是这种情况。

items.en: Answering a question requires understanding the entire context, but it may be possible to decompose a question without looking at the entire context. This allows us to apply Iterated Amplification to tasks where the context is too large for a human expert to observe directly. Instead, we can give H𝐻H the ability to look at small parts of the context as needed. Hopefully, H𝐻H can decompose a question into pieces that depend on smaller and smaller parts of the context, until arriving at questions that depend on only isolated facts from the context.
items.zh: 回答问题需要理解整个上下文，但可能可以在不查看整个上下文的情况下分解问题。这使得我们可以将迭代放大应用于人类专家无法直接观察的过大上下文任务。相反，我们可以让H根据需要查看上下文的微小部分。希望H能将问题分解为依赖于越来越小上下文部分的部分，直到得到仅依赖于上下文中孤立事实的问题。

items.en: Large contexts also facilitate an important trick for accelerating training. In many settings, almost all of the work of answering a question is actually about understanding the context, and it is possible to ask many different questions about a single context.
items.zh: 大上下文也有助于加速训练的一个重要技巧。在许多设置中，回答问题的几乎所有工作实际上都是理解上下文，并且可以针对单个上下文提出许多不同的问题。

items.en: We divide X𝑋X into two phases, a context-encoding phase and a question-answering phase. During training, rather than sampling a single question we sample a context together with multiple questions about that context. We reuse the work of the context-encoding phase across all of these questions. Similarly, when computing AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right), we reuse the context-encoding work between all of the subquestions that X𝑋X needs to answer. In our experiments, this speeds up training by an order of magnitude.
items.zh: 我们将X分为两个阶段：上下文编码阶段和问答阶段。在训练期间，我们不是采样单个问题，而是采样一个上下文以及关于该上下文的多个问题。我们在所有这些问题中重用上下文编码阶段的工作。类似地，在计算Amplifyᴴ(X)时，我们在X需要回答的所有子问题之间重用上下文编码工作。在我们的实验中，这使训练速度提高了一个数量级。

items.en: We implement X𝑋X as an encoder-decoder architecture with self-attention, closely following the Transformer architecture (Vaswani et al., 2017):
items.zh: 我们实现X为带有自注意力机制的编码器-解码器架构，紧密遵循Transformer架构（Vaswani等人，2017）：

items.en: We represent the context as a set of facts, each of which is a sequence of tokens.
items.zh: 我们将上下文表示为一组事实，每个事实是一个令牌序列。

items.en: We embed each token using a look-up table. We embed facts by concatenating the token embeddings and applying a linear projection.
items.zh: 我们使用查找表嵌入每个令牌。我们通过拼接令牌嵌入并应用线性投影来嵌入事实。

items.en: We apply the Transformer encoder to the embedded facts. Our only change to the architecture from (Vaswani et al., 2017) is inserting batchnorm after each MLP.
items.zh: 我们将Transformer编码器应用于嵌入的事实。我们对架构的唯一更改是在每个MLP之后插入批归一化。

items.en: We embed questions in the same way we embed facts, then apply the Transformer decoder to a batch of questions (omitting the self-attention altogether because it would correspond to interactions amongst questions, which ought to be independent).
items.zh: 我们以与嵌入事实相同的方式嵌入问题，然后将 Transformer 解码器应用于一批问题（完全省略自注意力机制，因为它对应于问题之间的交互，而这些问题应该是独立的）。

items.en: An autoregressive MLP generates a sequence of symbols conditioned on the result of the Transformer decoder. It generates symbols either by outputting a set of logits or by choosing to copy a symbol from the context (as in pointer networks (Vinyals et al., 2015)).
items.zh: 自回归 MLP 根据 Transformer 解码器的结果生成符号序列。它通过输出一组 logits 或选择从上下文中复制符号（如指针网络（Vinyals 等人，2015））来生成符号。

items.en: The human-predictor H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} is also a Transformer decoder augmented with the ability to copy symbols from previous steps. H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} operates on sequences of questions and answers—like H𝐻H, it never observes the entire context.
items.zh: 人类预测器 H′ 也是一个 Transformer 解码器，并增加了从先前步骤复制符号的能力。H′ 处理问题和答案的序列——与 H 一样，它从不观察整个上下文。

items.en: Details of our model architecture are described in Appendix D.
items.zh: 我们模型架构的详细信息见附录 D。

items.en: Expert Iteration: our method is very similar to Expert Iteration (ExIt) (Anthony et al., 2017) and AlphaZero (Silver et al., 2017b, a) and has recently achieved strong performance in the board games Hex, Go, Chess, and Shogi. ExIt is itself closely analogous to the Bellman update in Q learning, and all of these can be viewed as analogs of dynamic programming where neural networks replace lookup tables.
items.zh: 专家迭代：我们的方法与专家迭代（ExIt）（Anthony 等人，2017）和 AlphaZero（Silver 等人，2017b, a）非常相似，并且最近在棋盘游戏 Hex、围棋、国际象棋和将棋中取得了强劲表现。ExIt 本身与 Q 学习中的 Bellman 更新密切相关，所有这些都可以视为动态规划的类比，其中神经网络取代了查找表。

items.en: The key difference between our work and ExIt is the lack of an external objective. In ExIt, the expert is produced by a search algorithm that optimizes an external objective. Our contribution is to show that that a similar training process can be used even when the task definition is only implicit in the decomposition and recomposition strategy.
items.zh: 我们的工作与 ExIt 之间的关键区别在于缺乏外部目标。在 ExIt 中，专家由优化外部目标的搜索算法产生。我们的贡献在于表明，即使任务定义仅隐含在分解和重组策略中，也可以使用类似的训练过程。

items.en: Inverse reinforcement learning: by observing human behavior and inferring the underlying reward function that they are optimizing,(Ng et al., 2000; Hadfield-Menell et al., 2016) inverse reinforcement learning could also potentially learn reward functions for tasks that are too challenging for humans. Handling such tasks requires a sufficiently accurate model of human cognition to predict what humans “would” prefer if we relaxed their cognitive limitations; in addition to being extremely complex, such a model is not identifiable, because we never observe the ground truth about human preferences. Iterated Amplification is an alternative strategy that does not require solving this challenging model specification problem.
items.zh: 逆强化学习：通过观察人类行为并推断他们正在优化的潜在奖励函数，（Ng 等人，2000；Hadfield-Menell 等人，2016）逆强化学习也可能学习到对人类来说过于困难的任务的奖励函数。处理此类任务需要足够准确的人类认知模型，以预测如果放松认知限制，人类“会”偏好什么；除了极其复杂之外，这样的模型是不可识别的，因为我们从未观察到关于人类偏好的真实情况。迭代放大是一种替代策略，不需要解决这个具有挑战性的模型规范问题。

items.en: Debate: training AI systems to debate each other (Irving et al., 2018) is another possible approach to training question-answering systems where a human expert cannot evaluate answers directly. Both debate and Iterated Amplification involve a recursive structure where AI systems help humans address relevant subquestions. The largest conceptual difference is that in Iterated Amplification each subquestion is answered by an independent copy of X𝑋X trained by AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right), while in a debate the subquestions are answered by one of the debaters (who are trained to defend a particular answer to the top-level question).
items.zh: 辩论：训练 AI 系统相互辩论（Irving 等人，2018）是另一种可能的方法，用于训练人类专家无法直接评估答案的问答系统。辩论和迭代放大都涉及递归结构，其中 AI 系统帮助人类处理相关的子问题。最大的概念差异在于，在迭代放大中，每个子问题由通过 AmplifyH(X) 训练的独立 X 副本回答，而在辩论中，子问题由其中一位辩手（他们被训练为捍卫对顶层问题的特定答案）回答。

items.en: Algorithm learning: our problem differs from traditional work on learning algorithms (Graves et al., 2016; Kaiser and Sutskever, 2015; Neelakantan et al., 2015) because we don’t assume that we have access to ground truth labels.
items.zh: 算法学习：我们的问题不同于传统的算法学习工作（Graves 等人，2016；Kaiser 和 Sutskever，2015；Neelakantan 等人，2015），因为我们不假设可以访问真实标签。

items.en: Recursive model architectures: our work differs from recursive model architectures (Cai et al., 2017; Nowak and Bruna, 2016), in that the learned model doesn’t have a recursive structure. The recursive decomposition is used only to generate training data, and even then only a single step of decomposition is performed in each iteration.
items.zh: 递归模型架构：我们的工作不同于递归模型架构（Cai 等人，2017；Nowak 和 Bruna，2016），因为学习到的模型没有递归结构。递归分解仅用于生成训练数据，并且即使在那时，每次迭代也只执行一步分解。

items.en: So the trained agent might end up solving the task in a totally different way from the decomposition used by the human, and in particular it may learn heuristics that treat the problem holistically.
items.zh: 因此，训练后的智能体最终可能以与人类使用的分解完全不同的方式解决任务，特别是它可能学习整体处理问题的启发式方法。

items.en: This flexibility is important to the applicability of our method. It is often possible to divide a task into easier parts, but dividing interesting problems into pieces and solving them independently can be much less efficient than considering the problem holistically.
items.zh: 这种灵活性对于我们方法的适用性很重要。通常可以将任务划分为更简单的部分，但将有趣的问题分解成碎片并独立解决可能远不如整体考虑问题高效。

items.en: We study Iterated Amplification in a set of 5 toy algorithmic tasks. For each task, the agent X𝑋X is given a large combinatorial context and asked questions about that context:
items.zh: 我们在 5 个玩具算法任务中研究迭代放大。对于每个任务，智能体 X 被赋予一个大型组合上下文，并询问关于该上下文的问题：

items.en: Given a permutation σ:{1,…,64}→{1,…,64}:𝜎→1…641…64\sigma:\left\{1,\ldots,64\right\}\rightarrow\left\{1,\ldots,64\right\}, compute σk​(x)superscript𝜎𝑘𝑥\sigma^{k}\left(x\right) for k𝑘k up to 646464.
items.zh: 给定一个排列 σ:{1,…,64}→{1,…,64}，计算 σk(x)，其中 k 最大为 64。

items.en: Given a function f:{1,…,8}2→{1,…,8}:𝑓→superscript1…821…8f:\left\{1,\ldots,8\right\}^{2}\rightarrow\left\{1,\ldots,8\right\} and a sequence of 646464 assignments of the form x:=3assign𝑥3x:=3 or x:=f​(y,z)assign𝑥𝑓𝑦𝑧x:=f(y,z), evaluate a particular variable.
items.zh: 给定一个函数 f:{1,…,8}2→{1,…,8} 和一个由 64 个赋值组成的序列，形式为 x:=3 或 x:=f(y,z)，评估一个特定变量。

items.en: Given a function f:{0,1}6→{−1,0,1}:𝑓→superscript016101f:\left\{0,1\right\}^{6}\rightarrow\left\{-1,0,1\right\}, answer questions of the form “What is the sum of f​(x)𝑓𝑥f(x) over all x𝑥x matching the wildcard expression 0∗∗ 1∗∗0\ast\ast\;1\ast\ast?”
items.zh: 给定一个函数 f:{0,1}6→{−1,0,1}，回答诸如“所有匹配通配符表达式 0∗∗ 1∗∗ 的 x 的 f(x) 之和是多少？”的问题。

items.en: Given a directed graph with 646464 vertices and 128128128 edges, find the distance from s𝑠s to t𝑡t.
items.zh: 给定一个有 64 个顶点和 128 条边的有向图，找出从 s 到 t 的距离。

items.en: Given a rooted forest on 646464 vertices, find the root of the tree containing a vertex x𝑥x.
items.zh: 给定一个包含 64 个顶点的有根森林，找出包含顶点 x 的树的根。

items.en: More detailed descriptions of the tasks are available in Appendix C. We train each task using a curriculum of smaller instances, which is unrelated to our use of Iterated Amplification (supervised learning also needs a curriculum to learn these tasks in a reasonable amount of time even given ground truth labels).
items.zh: 任务的更详细描述见附录 C。我们使用较小实例的课程训练每个任务，这与我们使用迭代放大无关（即使给定真实标签，监督学习也需要课程才能在合理时间内学习这些任务）。

items.en: Rather than having a human perform the decomposition, we provide a hard-coded algorithm H𝐻H which decomposes each task (though we minimize the number of times we call this algorithm). Using these decompositions directly as a recursive algorithm is not efficient for any of the tasks.
items.zh: 我们没有让人类执行分解，而是提供了一个硬编码算法 H，该算法分解每个任务（尽管我们尽量减少调用该算法的次数）。直接将这些分解用作递归算法对于任何任务来说都不是高效的。

items.en: In order to evaluate Iterated Amplification, we compare it to supervised learning from the ground truth data. The results are presented in Fig. 2. Iterated Amplification is able to solve these tasks effectively with at worst a modest slowdown, achieving our main goal.
items.zh: 为了评估迭代放大，我们将其与基于真实数据的监督学习进行比较。结果如图2所示。迭代放大能够有效解决这些任务，最多只有适度的速度下降，实现了我们的主要目标。

items.en: The purpose of amplification is to handle tasks where an expert can perform decomposition but can’t solve the task directly. We don’t expect amplification to solve those tasks as quickly as supervised learning. Because we can learn these tasks almost as quickly as supervised learning from the ground truth, we have achieved our main goal.
items.zh: 放大的目的是处理那些专家可以执行分解但无法直接解决的任务。我们不期望放大能像监督学习那样快速地解决这些任务。由于我们几乎可以像基于真实数据的监督学习一样快地学习这些任务，因此我们实现了主要目标。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Comparison between Iterated Amplification (orange) and supervised learning from the ground truth data (blue). To better illustrate the dynamics of training, in Appendix A we compare the performance of X𝑋X and AmplifyH′⁡(X)superscriptAmplifysuperscriptH′𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H^{\prime}}}\left(X\right) separately for questions requiring different recursion depths.
items.capZh: 图2：迭代放大（橙色）与基于真实数据的监督学习（蓝色）的比较。为了更好地说明训练动态，在附录A中，我们分别比较了需要不同递归深度的问题上X和AmplifyH'(X)的性能。

items.en: In addition to requiring modestly more training steps, training with amplification requires about twice as much computation per question, because we need to actually generate the training targets by running AmplifyH′⁡(X)superscriptAmplifysuperscriptH′𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H^{\prime}}}\left(X\right).222Running AmplifyH′⁡(X)superscriptAmplifysuperscriptH′𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H^{\prime}}}\left(X\right) requires calling both X𝑋X and H′superscript𝐻′H^{\prime} between 3 and 10 times. We train on each (Q,A)𝑄𝐴(Q,A) pair about 10 times before removing it from the dataset. So the time required to generate a (Q,A)𝑄𝐴(Q,A) pair is comparable to the total time spent training on it, resulting in roughly twice the total computation per question. We trained in the same wall-clock time by generating data on a parallel worker.
items.zh: 除了需要稍多的训练步骤外，使用放大进行训练每个问题所需的计算量大约增加一倍，因为我们需要通过运行AmplifyH'(X)来实际生成训练目标。运行AmplifyH'(X)需要调用X和H'各3到10次。我们在每个(Q,A)对上训练约10次，然后将其从数据集中移除。因此，生成一个(Q,A)对所需的时间与在其上训练的总时间相当，导致每个问题的总计算量大约翻倍。我们通过并行工作器生成数据，在相同的挂钟时间内完成了训练。

items.en: Supervised learning required tens of millions of examples in order to learn these algorithms. This would be a prohibitive cost if the examples were provided by an external expert. In contrast, Iterated Amplification required tens of thousands of examples in order to learn the much simpler decompositions (numbers for each task are in Table 2 in Appendix A). The sample complexity will be much larger for realistic tasks, where modeling H𝐻H might require (for example) a high-quality language model. But the general phenomenon, that learning decompositions can be much cheaper than learning complex behaviors directly, is likely to hold for realistic tasks, since performing decomposition is strictly easier than solving the task without the aid of X𝑋X.
items.zh: 监督学习需要数千万个示例才能学习这些算法。如果这些示例由外部专家提供，成本将高得令人望而却步。相比之下，迭代放大只需要数万个示例就能学习更简单的分解（每个任务的数据见附录A表2）。对于现实任务，样本复杂度会大得多，因为建模H可能需要（例如）一个高质量的语言模型。但学习分解可能比直接学习复杂行为便宜得多这一普遍现象，很可能适用于现实任务，因为执行分解严格来说比在没有X帮助的情况下解决问题更容易。

items.en: These experiments make several important simplifications:
items.zh: 这些实验做了几个重要的简化：

items.en: In our experiments questions can be algorithmically decomposed into subquestions, and we replace the human with a hand-coded algorithm. These experiments don’t shed any light on whether humans can decompose interesting real world tasks, nor on whether it would be feasible to learn messy real world decompositions.
items.zh: 在我们的实验中，问题可以通过算法分解为子问题，并且我们用手工编码的算法代替了人类。这些实验并未阐明人类是否能够分解有趣的现实世界任务，也没有说明学习混乱的现实世界分解是否可行。

items.en: We work in combinatorial domains where it it is possible to construct an algorithmic training signal. This makes it possible for us to cheaply run experiments and easily evaluate our performance, but in the long run we care about tasks where it is not possible to construct an algorithmic or even human training signal.
items.zh: 我们在组合领域工作，在这些领域中，可以构建算法训练信号。这使得我们能够廉价地进行实验并轻松评估性能，但从长远来看，我们关心的是那些无法构建算法甚至人类训练信号的任务。

items.en: In our experiments X𝑋X is trained by supervised learning from AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right). In many important applications we suspect that we would learn a reward function from AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) and then train X𝑋X to maximize that reward function.
items.zh: 在我们的实验中，X通过从AmplifyH(X)进行监督学习来训练。在许多重要应用中，我们怀疑我们会从AmplifyH(X)学习一个奖励函数，然后训练X以最大化该奖励函数。

items.en: In order for Iterated Amplification to succeed, the question distribution 𝒟𝒟\mathcal{D} needs to be broad enough to cover not only the questions we care about, but also all of the subquestions asked during the computation of AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right). The distribution also determines how the model will allocate its capacity, and so must be carefully chosen. In our experiments, we started with a distribution 𝒟𝒟\mathcal{D} that could be used directly. In a more realistic setting, we might start with some distribution 𝒟0subscript𝒟0\mathcal{D}_{0} of questions that have intrinsic interest, and it would be the system designer’s responsibility to construct 𝒟𝒟\mathcal{D} appropriately.
items.zh: 为了使迭代放大成功，问题分布D需要足够广泛，不仅要涵盖我们关心的问题，还要涵盖在计算AmplifyH(X)过程中提出的所有子问题。该分布还决定了模型如何分配其容量，因此必须仔细选择。在我们的实验中，我们从一个可以直接使用的分布D开始。在更现实的环境中，我们可能会从一个具有内在兴趣的问题分布D0开始，系统设计者有责任适当地构建D。

items.en: Removing these simplifications is a task for future work, which will ultimately test the hypothesis that Iterated Amplification can be usefully applied to complex real-world tasks for which no other training strategy is available.
items.zh: 消除这些简化是未来工作的任务，最终将检验迭代放大是否可以有效地应用于没有其他训练策略可用的复杂现实世界任务这一假设。

full.sec: 5 Discussion of decomposition in realistic domains
full.secZh: 5 现实领域中的分解讨论

items.en: Having successfully applied Iterated Amplification to synthetic algorithmic problems, the natural question is whether it can actually be applied to complex real-world tasks that are “beyond human scale.” We leave a convincing demonstration to future work, but we discuss here why we think this is likely.
items.zh: 在成功地将迭代放大应用于合成算法问题之后，自然的问题是它是否真的可以应用于“超越人类尺度”的复杂现实世界任务。我们将令人信服的演示留给未来的工作，但在此讨论为什么我们认为这很可能。

items.en: The key assumption underlying Iterated Amplification is that a human can coordinate multiple copies of X𝑋X to perform better than a single copy of X𝑋X.
items.zh: 迭代放大的关键假设是，人类可以协调多个X副本以比单个X副本表现更好。

items.en: As an example, consider the problem of evaluating a proposed design for a transit system. Rather than forcing a single copy of X𝑋X to reach a snap judgment about a proposed design, we can have copies of X𝑋X evaluate many different considerations (estimating costs, evaluating how well the system serves different populations, and so on). A human can then decide how to aggregate those different considerations (potentially with help from further copies of X𝑋X). We flesh out this example in more detail in Appendix B.
items.zh: 例如，考虑评估一个拟议的交通系统设计的问题。与其强迫单个X副本对拟议设计做出快速判断，我们可以让X副本评估许多不同的考虑因素（估算成本、评估系统对不同人群的服务效果等）。然后，人类可以决定如何汇总这些不同的考虑因素（可能借助更多X副本的帮助）。我们在附录B中更详细地阐述了这一示例。

items.en: The problem of coordinating several copies of X𝑋X to outperform a single copy of X𝑋X is analogous to organizing a team of humans to outperform individual humans. Fortunately, there are several ways in which coordinating several copies of X𝑋X is easier than coordinating a team of humans:
items.zh: 协调多个X副本以超越单个X副本的问题类似于组织一个人类团队以超越个体人类。幸运的是，协调多个X副本比协调人类团队更容易，有以下几个原因：

items.en: We don’t require that the collaboration be efficient, it just needs to help at all. If ten agents working together perform “10% better” than a single agent on its own, then AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right) still provides a useful training signal that we can use to improve X𝑋X.
items.zh: 我们不要求协作高效，只需要它有所帮助。如果十个智能体一起工作比单个智能体单独工作“好10%”，那么AmplifyH(X)仍然提供了有用的训练信号，我们可以用它来改进X。

items.en: The copies of X𝑋X don’t need to run in parallel—each can start after the previous one has finished its task. Many tasks may be inherently difficult to parallelize, which is an obstacle for human collaboration but is fine for AmplifyH⁡(X)superscriptAmplifyH𝑋\operatorname{Amplify^{H}}\left(X\right).
items.zh: X的副本不需要并行运行——每个副本可以在前一个副本完成其任务后开始。许多任务可能本质上难以并行化，这对人类协作来说是一个障碍，但对AmplifyH(X)来说没有问题。

items.en: All of the copies of X𝑋X are trained exclusively to solve the problem they are given. We don’t need to manage incentives, politics, or conflicting preferences, which are common difficulties in human organizations.
items.zh: 所有X的副本都专门训练来解决它们被给予的问题。我们不需要管理激励、政治或冲突的偏好，这些是人类组织中常见的困难。

items.en: Despite these difficulties, human organizations are often able to significantly outperform individual humans in many domains, supporting our key assumption.
items.zh: 尽管存在这些困难，人类组织通常能够在许多领域显著超越个体人类，这支持了我们的关键假设。

items.en: We have shown that Iterated Amplification can successfully solve algorithmically complex tasks where there is no external reward function and the objective is implicit in a learned decomposition. This offers hope for applying ML in domains where we cannot compute a suitable objective, even with human help—as long as humans are able to decompose a task into simpler pieces. If we can realize this hope, it will be an important step towards expanding the reach of ML and addressing concerns about the long-term impacts of AI by reducing our reliance simple but inaccurate proxies for complex implicit objectives.
items.zh: 我们已经证明，迭代放大可以成功解决算法复杂的任务，这些任务没有外部奖励函数，目标隐含在学习到的分解中。这为在无法计算合适目标（即使有人类帮助）的领域应用机器学习带来了希望——只要人类能够将任务分解为更简单的部分。如果我们能够实现这一希望，那将是朝着扩展机器学习范围、减少对复杂隐含目标的简单但不准确代理的依赖、从而解决AI长期影响问题的重要一步。
